name: McCully / Moiliili
desc: Dense apartment district bridging Waikiki and UH — affordable rentals, walkable, diverse food, student-area energy, heavy traffic.
insider: Fukuya Delicatessen has the best mochi and Japanese deli items on Oahu — this family shop has been open since 1939
Pint + Jigger is a craft cocktail bar that most tourists never find — the bartenders are some of the best in Honolulu
McCully Shopping Center has Nijiya Market — the best Japanese grocery store on the island for bento, sashimi, and snacks
UH campus is open to walk through — the Japanese garden near the East-West Center is one of Honolulu's hidden peaceful spots
Rent here is 30-40% cheaper than Waikiki and you're only a 10-minute walk from the edge of Waikiki — best value hack in town
King Street through Moiliili has incredible Korean food — Camellia Buffet, Sorabol, and Yummy Korean BBQ are all solid
The Ala Wai Canal path is a popular jogging route from McCully to Waikiki — flat, paved, and busy with runners at dawn and dusk
Apartment buildings are old and dense — expect thin walls, limited parking, and window AC units in most units
Kokua Market on Beretania is Hawaii's only natural foods co-op — great for organic produce and bulk items
The intersection of King and University is a perpetual traffic bottleneck — avoid 4-6pm at all costs
